summary: 2026-07-07 went 10-6 directionally (62.5%) across 16 graded games (15 matchups, including both legs of the MIL@STL doubleheader), though only one pick actually cleared every hard cap and carried real Kelly stake: TEX ML PLATINUM, which won 8-3. That pick was DOWNGRADED to SKIP by Claude Brain using a stale pick_prob (0.781 at claude_picks fit-time, versus 0.695 in the final diag CSV) that no longer supported the CAP9-adjacent rationale used to downgrade it. HARD CAP 9 scored its most decisive win of the archive on the other end: LAD's 0.9501 pick_prob (the single highest reading logged all season) lost outright to COL 4-3. Losses at HOU@WSH and CLE@MIN were well-supported by pre-cap signal stacks that simply lost to variance; TOR@SF and ARI@SD losses were weak, PQI/SP-against or signal-light stacks that behaved as expected. A first-time-observed data-quality issue: the pick_prob quoted inside HARD CAP 9/10 grade_reasons text differs from the live pick_prob CSV column on almost every capped row this slate.

MIL @ STL (G1).headline: Misiorowski and MIL held on late to beat STL 4-3 in the doubleheader opener; model pick correct.
MIL @ STL (G1).hypothesis: Pre-cap score was 4 (F1_xera_gap=5.58, F3_swing_take_gap=2230.3, PQI +15.9 all supporting MIL) before HARD CAP 10 zeroed the score on the bottom-bucket pick_prob (0.5253); odds_status=no_match meant Kelly stake was 0 regardless, purely a data-availability gap, not a caps judgment. Claude's claude_picks entry for 'MIL @ STL' predates STL's SP announcement (recorded PENDING_SP_DATA/TBD), so it never actually reviewed this specific DIAMOND-tier pick — direction was correct but untested by the qualitative layer.
MIL @ STL (G1).signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket marginal-favorite — this game also shows the pick_prob-mismatch data-quality issue (grade_reasons cites 0.528 vs live column 0.5253)

MIL @ STL (G2).headline: MIL routed STL 10-2 in game two behind Gasser; model pick correct.
MIL @ STL (G2).hypothesis: Pre-cap score was 5 (PLATINUM tier bonus + F-signal + PQI +15.9, all agreeing) before HARD CAP 10 zeroed it on the bottom-bucket pick_prob (0.5073); odds_status=no_match again meant zero stake independent of the cap. Same PENDING_SP_DATA timing gap as game one — Claude's recorded decision was on a placeholder pick, not this PLATINUM-tier read.
MIL @ STL (G2).signals_to_recheck: PENDING_SP_DATA games resolving into DIAMOND/PLATINUM tiers after Claude's review window closes — see patterns_observed

CHC @ BAL.headline: CHC beat BAL 5-2; thin-edge SKIP pick still landed on the right side.
CHC @ BAL.hypothesis: Edge was essentially flat (+0.54pp, inside HARD CAP 10's bottom bucket at pick_prob=0.5171) with no F-signal or SP-edge dimension in grade_reasons — a coin-flip read that happened to hit. Variance, not process: nothing in the stack argued strongly for CHC beyond a marginal Stage 1/2 agreement.
CHC @ BAL.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 10] bottom-bucket marginal-favorite (31.2% archive non-CONFIRM hit rate) — today's CONFIRM landed, keep tracking against that baseline

OAK @ DET.headline: DET beat OAK 6-2 behind Skubal; GOLD pick correct but zero-staked on negative edge.
OAK @ DET.hypothesis: Pre-cap stack was strong (F1*, F3, SP edge 0.82 xERA, PQI +8.8 all for DET) but a -14.22pp negative edge capped the grade at C and zeroed Kelly stake before HARD CAP 10 even applied. This is another data point for the archive's 'negative edge is an EV filter, not a direction filter' finding — DET's win doesn't argue for chasing negative-edge GOLD picks, it just confirms capital was correctly protected on a coin-flip-priced favorite.
OAK @ DET.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1]-style negative edge cap (-14.2pp) — also affected by the pick_prob-mismatch bug: live pick_prob=0.5582 sits outside the (0.50,0.55] HARD CAP 10 bucket the grade_reasons text cites (0.533)

ATL @ PIT.headline: PIT blew out ATL 12-4 behind Skenes; SKIP pick correct, edge just too small to stake.
ATL @ PIT.hypothesis: Pre-cap B+ (score 3): moderate F3, PQI +18.4, Stage 1/2 agreement, but edge (+1.71pp) fell outside the stakeable [4,15]pp band, so this was never eligible for real money regardless of caps. Process was sound; market had priced Skenes efficiently even as PIT blew the game open.
ATL @ PIT.signals_to_recheck: None — clean SKIP-band read that hit; no cap fired

SEA @ MIA.headline: MIA edged SEA 6-5; pick correct despite the model's own signals arguing against it.
SEA @ MIA.hypothesis: Grade was D (score -2): SP edge (0.41 xERA) and PQI (Δ=-7.2) both ran AGAINST MIA, with only a weak Stage 1/2 agreement in its favor. MIA winning anyway is a clean variance case — the model's own dimensions actively opposed the pick, so this isn't validation of the read, just a game that broke the other way.
SEA @ MIA.signals_to_recheck: SP-xERA-against + PQI-against dual-negative stack (D-grade) — archive treats this as historically volatile; today's result doesn't resolve that either way

NYY @ TB.headline: TB beat NYY 6-4; SKIP pick correct.
NYY @ TB.hypothesis: Pipeline tier held SKIP despite a raw A-looking stack (SP edge 0.71 xERA, PQI +12.2, Stage 1/2 agree) because of bullpen_fatigue/confidence_downgrade flags and a starred F1 (1.03*) without F3 corroboration — the archive's F1*-without-F3 caution. TB winning anyway is consistent with the underlying signals being directionally fine even though the caution flags correctly kept this unstaked (edge -1.57pp also triggered HARD CAP 1).
NYY @ TB.signals_to_recheck: F1_star_no_secondary_signal caution — direction was fine today, cap discipline (HARD CAP 1 at -1.57pp, HARD CAP 10) still correctly zeroed the stake

HOU @ WSH.headline: HOU upset WSH 6-3 on the road; a coherent pre-cap stack lost to variance.
HOU @ WSH.hypothesis: Pre-cap score was 4 (B+ range): SP edge 0.83 xERA, PQI +8.5, Stage 1/2 agreement, and team_quality modifier all pointed to WSH, then HARD CAP 1 (-2.66pp negative edge) and HARD CAP 10 correctly zeroed the stake. This reads as a variance failure, not a process failure — the signal stack was coherent and the caps already protected capital; HOU simply won on the field.
HOU @ WSH.signals_to_recheck: negative_edge_GOLD ([HARD CAP 1] at -2.66pp) — capital was protected; no process fix indicated

KC @ NYM.headline: KC outslugged NYM 16-12; SKIP pick correct in a high-scoring game.
KC @ NYM.hypothesis: Pre-cap score 5 (SP edge 0.67 xERA, F-signal, PQI all for KC) before HARD CAP 7 (pick-side bullpen disadvantage, hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.016) and HARD CAP 10 zeroed it. KC winning in a bullpen-heavy shootout (16-12) is a mild counterpoint to CAP 7's bullpen-disadvantage logic today, though the game likely turned on offense more than pen quality.
KC @ NYM.signals_to_recheck: hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap ([HARD CAP 7] fired at +0.016) — direction was still correct despite the bullpen-disadvantage flag, add to the ongoing CAP 7 near-zero-gap threshold debate

PHI @ CIN.headline: PHI beat CIN 4-1; every available signal argued against this pick and it lost as expected.
PHI @ CIN.hypothesis: Process failure avoided, not committed: SP edge (1.91 xERA) and PQI (Δ=-38.2) both ran hard AGAINST CIN, and HARD CAP 6 fired on a +27.85pp edge in the calibrator-hallucination range (>25pp, implausible in MLB closing lines). Every dimension pointed away from CIN and Wheeler beat CIN as expected — a clean validation of HARD CAP 6 and the against-signal stack, correctly left unstaked.
PHI @ CIN.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 6] extreme_positive_edge_hallucination (+27.85pp) — validated cleanly, no action needed beyond continued enforcement

CLE @ MIN.headline: MIN beat CLE 3-1 at home; a modest pre-cap stack lost to variance.
CLE @ MIN.hypothesis: Pre-cap score was 3: F-signal, PQI (Δ=-3.7), and Stage 1/2 agreement supported CLE, but no SP-edge dimension appeared in grade_reasons (missing corroboration), and HARD CAP 10 zeroed the score on the bottom-bucket pick_prob (0.5026). This reprises the archive's 'grade lacking SP-xERA dimension is a vulnerable stack' pattern (SD@BAL, 2026-06-13) — worth reinforcing rather than treating as a fresh finding.
CLE @ MIN.signals_to_recheck: Grade stacks missing an SP-xERA dimension (reconfirms 2026-06-13 SD@BAL pattern) — treat as a standing vulnerability, not new information

BOS @ CHW.headline: BOS crushed CHW 8-1 behind Schultz; DIAMOND pick correct but zero-staked on negative edge.
BOS @ CHW.hypothesis: The strongest pre-cap stack of the slate outside TEX (F1=3.12, F3=7838.9, F5_bullpen=+0.65, SP edge 2.29 xERA) still tripped HARD CAP 1 on a -2.32pp negative edge, then HARD CAP 10. BOS winning big reinforces the archive's standing conclusion that HARD CAP 1 is an EV filter, not a direction filter — the market had already priced Schultz's edge even though BOS's underlying quality gap was real and showed up on the scoreboard.
BOS @ CHW.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 1] negative-edge discipline on elite triple-F stacks — still correctly EV-neutral, no change indicated

LAA @ TEX.headline: TEX beat LAA 8-3 behind deGrom; the slate's only cap-clean, actually-staked pick hit — and Claude downgraded it to SKIP.
LAA @ TEX.hypothesis: Process failure: this was the sole matchup with a pre-cap score of 10 (grade A) and nonzero Kelly stakes (kelly_full=0.2283) — no hard cap fired. Claude's claude_picks entry recorded pick_prob=0.781 as 'just under the CAP 9 top-bucket cutoff' and downgraded to SKIP on that basis, but the final diag CSV shows pick_prob=0.695, comfortably outside the CAP9-adjacent danger zone the downgrade relied on. The probability moved meaningfully between claude_picks fit-time and the diag CSV lock, and the downgrade rationale was never revisited against the updated number — a real, quantifiable cost today since this was the one pick that would have been profitably staked.
LAA @ TEX.signals_to_recheck: Stage_1_2_disagree_with_confidence_downgrade cited by Claude at fit-time (pick_prob=0.781) no longer matched the diag CSV's live pick_prob=0.695 — see patterns_observed on probability drift between claude_picks and the diag CSV

ARI @ SD.headline: SD beat ARI 4-1 at home; a signal-light stack lost as expected.
ARI @ SD.hypothesis: Pre-cap score was only 1: no F-signal, no SP-xERA dimension, just team_quality and a loose Stage 1/2 agreement — the same 'vulnerable stack' shape flagged in the archive (SD@BAL, 2026-06-13). HARD CAP 10 zeroed the score regardless. This reads as a clean process validation: a weak stack lost, consistent with prior findings, not a new failure mode.
ARI @ SD.signals_to_recheck: Grade lacking SP-xERA dimension (reconfirms 2026-06-13 SD@BAL pattern) for the second time this slate (also CLE@MIN)

TOR @ SF.headline: TOR blew out SF 9-3 on the road; the model's own against-signals correctly called this one wrong for SF.
TOR @ SF.hypothesis: Grade was D (score -2): SP edge (0.59 xERA) and PQI (Δ=-17.7) both ran AGAINST the SF pick. TOR's blowout win confirms the against-signal read was correct and the SKIP/no-stake outcome was the right call — a clean process validation, not a failure.
TOR @ SF.signals_to_recheck: None — dual-against D-grade correctly predicted the loss, no action needed

COL @ LAD.headline: COL stunned LAD 4-3 as a road underdog against a 0.95 model favorite — the archive's most decisive HARD CAP 9 validation yet.
COL @ LAD.hypothesis: Process win: pick_prob reached 0.9501 (the highest single-game reading referenced anywhere in the postgame archive), with a large Stage 1/2 disagreement (f5=0.7068 vs full=0.9501, Δ=0.2433) that is the textbook calibration-hallucination shape. Both HARD CAP 3 (PLATINUM/DIAMOND calibration artifact) and HARD CAP 9 (top-bucket hallucination) fired and zeroed the score; Claude independently DOWNGRADED to SKIP. COL's outright upset win is as strong a confirmation as the archive has seen for treating extreme top-bucket favorites with more suspicion, not less.
COL @ LAD.signals_to_recheck: [HARD CAP 9] top-bucket calibrator hallucination — also affected by the pick_prob-mismatch bug: grade_reasons text cites pick_prob=0.873 while the live CSV column shows 0.9501 (both clear the >0.80 threshold, so the cap outcome itself was unaffected here)

patterns_observed: Claude Brain's LAA@TEX DOWNGRADE relied on pick_prob=0.781 recorded at claude_picks fit-time (cited as 'just under the CAP 9 cutoff'), but the final diag CSV showed pick_prob=0.695 — comfortably clear of that zone. The probability drifted between fit-time and the diag CSV lock and the downgrade was never revisited; this turned the slate's only cap-clean, actually-staked pick (grade A, score 10, no caps fired) into a non-bet, and it won 8-3. Going forward, DOWNGRADE/OVERRIDE rationale built on a probability near a hard-cap boundary should be re-checked against the diag CSV's live pick_prob before being finalized.

The pick_prob value quoted inside HARD CAP 9/10/3 grade_reasons text does not match the live pick_prob CSV column on nearly every capped row this slate (e.g. OAK@DET: text cites 0.533, live column is 0.5582; ARI@SD: text cites 0.533, live column is 0.5603 — both live values actually sit outside the (0.50,0.55] HARD CAP 10 bottom-bucket band the cached text claims triggered the cap). The cap outcomes were not overturned by this today, but any close boundary case going forward should be verified against the live pick_prob column, not the cached grade_reasons string.

HARD CAP 9's top-bucket calibration-hallucination pattern scored its most extreme validation yet: COL@LAD's pick_prob=0.9501 for LAD (the highest single-game reading referenced in the postgame archive) lost outright to a division-rival underdog, 4-3. Treat pick_prob>0.90 with at least as much suspicion as the established 0.80-0.90 archive band, not less.

Two PENDING_SP_DATA games (both legs of the MIL@STL doubleheader) resolved into DIAMOND/PLATINUM-tier picks after Claude Brain's review window had already closed, so neither pick ever received a qualitative check — both happened to win today, but this is a structural gap (Claude reviews before SP announcements land, the pipeline grades after), not a validated process. Flag PENDING_SP_DATA games that resolve into DIAMOND/PLATINUM tiers for same-day re-review when possible.
